metadata.title: Data-efficient Large Vision Models through Sequential Autoregression
organizations: 1School of information and Electronics, Beijing Institute of Technology.
2School of Computer Science, Faculty of Engineering, The University of Sydney.

sections.title: Introduction
content: Training general-purpose large multimodal foundation models on purely sequential data, similar to linguistic inputs, has heralded a new frontier in visual perception. However, current endeavors are hamstrung by an over-reliance on colossal models, exemplified by the necessity for an extensive corpus of data, often comprising staggering 400B tokens. In this work, we delve into the development of an efficient, autoregression-based foundation model, innovatively architected to operate on a limited dataset.

figures.description: Overview of the proposed autoregressive model training, inference, and data augmentation/distillation strategies

sections.title: Part-1: Data-efficient Training: Data Augmentation Strategy
content: Dataset augmentation yielded the best overall performance.

figures.description: Validation loss and perplexity curves for single task image segmentation and pose estimation

sections.title: Part-2: Low Power Consumption & Tiny Model: Knowledge Distillation
content: The introduction of KD proves beneficial in enhancing the single-task performance of LVMs.
The introduction of KD proves beneficial in enhancing the multi-task performance of LVMs.

sections.title: Beyond Perception: Generation Ability
content: Next frame prediction: zoom in, zoom out, camera motion.
